allow:
Bash(cd "N:/git/eclipse/fennec/osgi.emf" && gh issue create --title "Security: SSRF via RestfulURIHandlerImpl - no URL validation" --label "security,bug" --body "$\(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

`RestfulURIHandlerImpl` opens HTTP/HTTPS connections to arbitrary URLs without any allowlist, blocklist, or validation. Any EMF URI with an `http://` or `https://` scheme is directly opened as a network connection.

## Severity

**High** â€” OWASP A10:2021 \(Server-Side Request Forgery\)

## Location

- `org.eclipse.fennec.emf.osgi/src/org/eclipse/fennec/emf/osgi/urihandler/RestfulURIHandlerImpl.java`
- Affected methods: `createOutputStream\(\)` \(line 85-86\), `createInputStream\(\)` \(line 162-163\), `delete\(\)` \(line 266-267\), `getAttributes\(\)` \(line 304\), `exists\(\)` \(line 373-374\)

## Description

Every method in `RestfulURIHandlerImpl` converts an EMF `URI` to a `java.net.URI` and opens an `HttpURLConnection` without any URL validation:

```java
java.net.URI netUri = java.net.URI.create\(uri.toString\(\)\);
final HttpURLConnection httpURLConnection = \(HttpURLConnection\) netUri.toURL\(\).openConnection\(\);
```

Since this handler is registered for all HTTP/HTTPS URIs via `ResourceSetUriHandlerConfiguratorComponent`, any code that loads an EMF model from a user-controlled URI can trigger arbitrary HTTP requests to internal services.

## Attack Scenario

An attacker provides a URI like `http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/` \(AWS metadata endpoint\) or `http://internal-service:8080/admin` through any mechanism that creates EMF resources with user-supplied URIs \(e.g., via `DynamicPackageLoader`'s `dynamicEcoreUri` configuration\). The framework would issue HTTP requests to those internal endpoints.

## Recommended Fix

- Add configurable URI allowlist/blocklist
- Block private/internal IP ranges \(10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x, localhost, IPv6 link-local\)
- Consider resolving hostnames before connecting and checking the resolved IP against blocklists
EOF
\)" 2>&1)

Bash(cd "N:/git/eclipse/fennec/osgi.emf" && gh label create security --color D93F0B --description "Security vulnerability" 2>&1 && gh label create high-severity --color B60205 --description "High severity issue" 2>&1 && gh label create medium-severity --color D93F0B --description "Medium severity issue" 2>&1 && gh label create low-severity --color E4E669 --description "Low severity issue" 2>&1)
